source_pr.title: planner: return error when the item of ORDER BY is hidden column
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #26214 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
If the item of ORDER BY is hidden column, TiDB should return `Unknown column xxx in 'order clause'` but it didn't.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed & How it Works:
In `positionToScalarFunc`, check whether the column that `PositionExpr` refers to is hidden column. If yes, return error.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [x] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory

Documentation

- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- return error when the item of ORDER BY is hidden column<!-- Please write a release note here to describe the change you made when it is released to the users of TiDB. If your PR doesn't involve any change to TiDB(like test enhancements, RFC proposals...), you can write `No release note`. -->

symptoms: _, err := tk.Exec("select * from t where case when a < 0 then 1 else 2 end <= 1 order by 4;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: _, err := tk.Exec("select * from t where case when a < 0 then 1 else 2 end <= 1 order by 4;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: _, err := tk.Exec("select * from t where case when a < 0 then 1 else 2 end <= 1 order by 4;") (planner/core/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: _, err := tk.Exec("select * from t where case when a < 0 then 1 else 2 end <= 1 order by 4;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: _, err := tk.Exec("select * from t where case when a < 0 then 1 else 2 end <= 1 order by 4;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
